RISK-002.title: Xmlrpc enabled Leads to Ping back attack
RISK-002.summary: XML-RPC enabled allows distributed pingback amplification attacks, enabling DDoS via pingback.ping method.

components.label: Enable protection for Xmlrpc enabled Leads to Ping back attack

.htaccess.note: Apache 2.4+: use Require all denied

operation_notes.what_this_does: Disable XML-RPC via .htaccess or plugins like Disable XML-RPC. Block /xmlrpc.php entirely.
operation_notes.balanced_protection: BALANCED: Blocks multicall brute-force and pingback exploitation only. Legitimate XML-RPC callers (Jetpack, WordPress mobile apps, ManageWP, MainWP, UpdraftPlus, WP CLI remote) continue to work. To completely disable XML-RPC when no integrations require it, add: <Files xmlrpc.php> Require all denied </Files> — but verify no plugins use it first (check with: grep -r xmlrpc wp-content/plugins).

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_rewrite loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep rewrite
Verify mod_setenvif loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep setenvif
Verify AllowOverride includes 'FileInfo' in httpd.conf or VirtualHost block
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps: Open .htaccess in editor (create if it does not exist)

Add the following block at the top of the file:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-002
# Block XML-RPC pingback method exploitation only
# Allows: Jetpack, mobile apps, ManageWP, authenticated XML-RPC clients
# Blocks: pingback flood attacks and multicall brute-force
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteBase /
    # Block multicall brute-force (common attack vector)
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/xmlrpc.php$ [NC]
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_METHOD} POST [NC]
    RewriteCond %{THE_REQUEST} system\.multicall [NC]
    RewriteRule ^ - [F,L]
</IfModule>
# Block pingback user-agent (WordPress pingback bots)
<IfModule mod_setenvif.c>
    SetEnvIf HTTP_USER_AGENT "WordPress/.*" XMLRPC_PINGBACK
    SetEnvIfExpr "req_novary('X-Pingback') != ''" XMLRPC_PINGBACK
</IfModule>
# END VAPT RISK-002

automated_verification.expected_result: HTTP 403 for multicall attack, HTTP 200 for legitimate POST

RISK-003.title: Username Enumeration via WordPress REST API
RISK-003.summary: WP REST API /wp/v2/users endpoint exposes usernames without authentication.

components.label: Enable protection for Username Enumeration via WordPress REST API

.htaccess.note: Must be BEFORE # BEGIN WordPress. WP [L] catch-all makes anything after # END WordPress a dead zone. Covers /users and /users/ variants.

operation_notes.what_this_does: Add .htaccess rule: RewriteRule ^wp-json/wp/v2/users - [F]. Use authentication plugins.
operation_notes.balanced_protection: BALANCED: Blocks unauthenticated enumeration only. Authenticated requests (Authorization header, WordPress login cookie) pass through unaffected. This preserves: Gutenberg editor @mention autocomplete, WooCommerce admin panel, JWT auth plugins, REST API admin clients. ALSO RECOMMENDED: Add via PHP using rest_api_init to set permission_callback on users endpoint — this is more reliable than .htaccess for cookie-based auth detection. See RISK-003.php_functions for the hook-based alternative.
operation_notes.placement_critical: MUST be placed BEFORE # BEGIN WordPress. WordPress ends its rewrite block with RewriteRule . /index.php [L] — this [L] flag halts all further rewrite processing, making anything after # END WordPress a silent dead zone.

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_rewrite loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep rewrite
Verify AllowOverride includes 'FileInfo' in httpd.conf or VirtualHost block
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps:
Insert the following block IMMEDIATELY BEFORE that line:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-003
# Require authentication for /wp-json/wp/v2/users endpoint
# Allows: Gutenberg @mentions, WooCommerce admin, JWT auth, logged-in admins
# Blocks: unauthenticated user enumeration by bots and scanners
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteBase /
    # Block unauthenticated requests to /users endpoint
    # Passes through: requests with Authorization header (JWT, Basic, OAuth)
    # Passes through: requests with WordPress auth cookies (logged-in users)
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/wp-json/wp/v2/users [NC]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:Authorization} ^$ [NC]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP_COOKIE} !wordpress_logged_in [NC]
    RewriteCond %{QUERY_STRING} !rest_route [NC]
    RewriteRule ^ - [F,L]
</IfModule>
# END VAPT RISK-003

automated_verification.expected_result: HTTP 403 for unauthenticated, HTTP 200 with valid Authorization header

components.label: Enable protection for Sensitive Files Accessible

.htaccess.note: Apache 2.4+: use Require all denied

operation_notes.what_this_does: Adds .htaccess directive to protect against: Sensitive Files Accessible
operation_notes.balanced_protection: BALANCED: Removed .yml and .conf from block list — many WordPress plugins and themes use these for legitimate configuration (DDEV, Docker Compose, WP CLI). .log files moved to RISK-030 for cleaner separation. Added .htpasswd to block list. Apache 2.4+: use Require all denied instead of Order/Deny.

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_authz_host loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep authz_host
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps: Open .htaccess in editor (create if it does not exist)

Add the following block at the top of the file:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-019
# Block access to sensitive configuration and data files
# Preserves: WordPress core files, plugin/theme CSS/JS/PHP, REST API, admin area
<FilesMatch "\.(env|sql|bak|backup|old|orig|save|swp|dump|htpasswd)$|^\.git$|^\.gitignore$">
    Order Deny,Allow
    Deny from all
</FilesMatch>
# END VAPT RISK-019
functional_checks: curl -sI 'https://yoursite.com/blocked-file' | grep HTTP   # expect 403

evidence_to_collect.description: Configuration showing Sensitive Files Accessible protection

RISK-023.title: Trace Method Enabled
RISK-023.summary: HTTP TRACE method allows XSS attacks.

components.label: Enable protection for Trace Method Enabled

.htaccess.note: TraceEnable off is server-level only (silently ignored in .htaccess). Also add: TraceEnable off to httpd.conf.

operation_notes.what_this_does: Adds .htaccess directive to protect against: Trace Method Enabled
operation_notes.deployment_note: TraceEnable off is server-level only (silently ignored in .htaccess). Also add: TraceEnable off to httpd.conf.
operation_notes.placement_critical: MUST be placed BEFORE # BEGIN WordPress. WordPress ends its rewrite block with RewriteRule . /index.php [L] — this [L] flag halts all further rewrite processing, making anything after # END WordPress a silent dead zone.

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_rewrite loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep rewrite
Verify AllowOverride includes 'FileInfo' in httpd.conf or VirtualHost block
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps:
Insert the following block IMMEDIATELY BEFORE that line:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-023
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteBase /
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_METHOD} ^TRACE [NC]
    RewriteRule .* - [F,L]
</IfModule>
# END VAPT RISK-023
functional_checks: tail -f /var/log/apache2/error.log — confirm no 500 errors after applying

.htaccess.note: Apache 2.4+: use Require all denied

operation_notes.what_this_does: Adds .htaccess directive to protect against: WordPress Install File Accessible
operation_notes.balanced_protection: BALANCED: Restricts to localhost only rather than global block. This preserves access during multisite sub-site provisioning from the server itself. To run a fresh install: temporarily comment out this block, install, then re-enable. For managed hosting: add your admin IP to the Allow list. Apache 2.4+: replace Order/Deny/Allow with: Require local

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_authz_host loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep authz_host
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps: Open .htaccess in editor (create if it does not exist)

Add the following block at the top of the file:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-027
# Restrict wp-admin/install.php to local access only
# Remove this rule temporarily if running a fresh WordPress install
<Files "install.php">
    Order Deny,Allow
    Deny from all
    Allow from 127.0.0.1

operation_notes.what_this_does: Adds .htaccess directive to protect against: WordPress Upgrade File Accessible
operation_notes.balanced_protection: BALANCED: Restricts to localhost rather than global block. WordPress auto-updates execute as the web server process (127.0.0.1 passes). Manual updates from wp-admin also execute server-side and pass. External wp-cli upgrade: run from the server itself (127.0.0.1). For remote admin access: add your static IP to the Allow list. Apache 2.4+: replace with: Require local

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_authz_host loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep authz_host
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
application_steps: Open .htaccess in editor (create if it does not exist)

Add the following block at the top of the file:

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-028
# Restrict wp-admin/upgrade.php to authenticated admin sessions and local access
# Allows: WordPress auto-updater (runs as admin), wp-cli upgrade, manual core updates
# Blocks: unauthenticated external access to upgrade endpoint
<Files "upgrade.php">
    Order Deny,Allow
    Deny from all
    Allow from 127.0.0.1
    Allow from ::1
    # Add your admin IP: Allow from YOUR.ADMIN.IP.HERE
</Files>
# END VAPT RISK-028
functional_checks: curl -sI 'https://yoursite.com/upgrade.php' | grep HTTP   # expect 403

operation_notes.what_this_does: Adds .htaccess directive to protect against: Apache Status Page Accessible
operation_notes.deployment_note: Preferred: restrict in httpd.conf with <Location /server-status> Require local </Location>
operation_notes.placement_critical: MUST be placed BEFORE # BEGIN WordPress. WordPress ends its rewrite block with RewriteRule . /index.php [L] — this [L] flag halts all further rewrite processing, making anything after # END WordPress a silent dead zone.

pre_application_checks: Verify mod_rewrite loaded: apache2ctl -M 2>/dev/null | grep rewrite
Verify AllowOverride includes 'FileInfo' in httpd.conf or VirtualHost block
Back up: cp .htaccess .htaccess.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)

# BEGIN VAPT RISK-035
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteBase /
    RewriteRule ^server-status - [F,L]
    RewriteRule ^server-info - [F,L]
</IfModule>
# END VAPT RISK-035
